General Times
Sign-On / Sign-Off15 mins
Unit Preparation (3-car)20 mins
Unit Preparation (6-car)40 mins
Pre-departure test only10 mins
Stow train10 mins
Turnback (with turnback driver)3 mins
Turnback (without turnback driver)8 mins
Max turnback/dwell at platform (no meal facility)20 mins
Max dwell — non-platform, no toilet units90 mins
Meal Periods
Shift ≤ 7 hrs 30 mins40 mins
Shift > 7 hrs 30 mins — 1st break30 mins
Shift > 7 hrs 30 mins — 2nd break30 mins
Non-revenue / shunt / NGR reposition60 mins
Shifts >7h30: 1st break commences 2½–3½ hrs in; 2nd break commences 5–6½ hrs in. Minimum 2 hrs between end of 1st and start of 2nd break.
Set radio function to TCR to access talkgroups. Use correct channel per General Appendix MD-14-36.

❓
Help Guide
TRAINS Guard Console
First Login
1
Open the app
Open Safari and go to slyz1967-hub.github.io/trains-qr/index.html — no installation required.
Tip: Share → Add to Home Screen for instant one-tap access and offline use.
2
Enter Service Number and PIN
Enter your 6-digit service number → Next → 4-digit PIN → Sign In. Credentials provided by your supervisor.
Forgotten your PIN? Ask your supervisor to reset it via the Supervisor Dashboard. The app will tell you specifically whether your service number or PIN is wrong.
3
Set your Roster Week
The app calculates your current roster week automatically. If wrong, tap My Roster from Quick Actions and adjust manually.
4
Check your Job Card
Tap My Job Card from Quick Actions to see sign on/off times, workings, and meal breaks. To swap, tap My Roster → tap the day → select job card → Regular or Overtime → Save.
5
Quick Actions & Reference & Tools
The home screen has two grids:

Quick Actions — your 6 operational buttons: Report Incident, Report Fault, My Roster, My Job Card, View Reports, Notices.

Reference & Tools — utility items used less frequently: Dark Mode, Google Maps, BOM Weather, TransLink, NextThere, Help Guide.
6
Set Dark Mode if preferred
Tap 🌙 Dark Mode from the Reference & Tools section. Tap ☀️ Light Mode to switch back. Preference is saved automatically across sessions.
7
Session timeout
The app signs you out automatically after 15 minutes of inactivity. A subtle amber banner appears at the 5-minute mark — tap anywhere to stay signed in. A countdown modal appears at 60 seconds. Tap I'm still here to continue your session.
Navigation
🏠
Home
Summary, quick actions, current working
🗒️
Run Report

Job Cards
Browse all job cards
❓
Help
This guide
Online / Offline: The app works fully offline. Reports and run sheets save locally and sync when back online. Red banner = offline.
Home Screen Features
★
Emergency & Roster contacts
Always visible below Quick Actions. Tap any row to dial directly. RMC Emergency 1800 079 303 is at the top in red — one tap in an emergency.
📓
My Diary
Tap 📓 Diary on the home screen to view all past shifts — incidents, faults, late running reports, boarding assists, and shift notes. Saves automatically at sign-off for every shift type including trackwork and special runs. Search by job card, date, or incident description. Tap 📤 Export to copy or share a shift summary.
★
Operational Reference panels
Scroll past Today's Summary to find collapsible reference panels:

⏱️ SEQ Time Allocations — sign-on/off, unit prep, turnbacks, meal periods
📡 UTC Radio Zones — all 11 zones with coverage and phone numbers
🚆 Rollingstock Reference — all 8 fleet types: units, speed, toilet, capacity
🚶 Change Train Walk Times — full official walk time table
🏭 Bowen Hills & Mayne Walk Times — BHI, MNE, ETS/ETF/ETD, YNY
🗺️ Passenger Information — key interchanges and accessibility overview for major stations
🎒 Lost Property Log — record found items with train number, carriage, and who it was handed to. Saves to your shift diary
♿ Station Accessibility — searchable list of all SEQ stations showing lift and ramp availability
📞
RMC Emergency from Run Sheet
The Quick Log bar at the bottom of every run sheet now includes a 📞 RMC button — one tap dials RMC Emergency (1800 079 303) without leaving the run sheet.
🚑
Medical Emergency quick reference
When logging an incident, select Medical as the type — a quick reference card appears immediately with the 7-step response checklist and a one-tap call button to RMC Emergency.
⚠️
Near-Miss reporting
Near-Miss is now an incident type in the incident form. Use it to record situations that could have led to an incident but didn't — helps supervisors identify patterns before they become safety events.
📋
Handover summary on shift card
When you log in and accept a handover, a summary banner appears below your shift card showing the outgoing guard's incidents, faults, late runs, and shift notes at a glance. It stays visible throughout your shift so you don't need to remember what was in the modal. Tap ✕ Dismiss when you're done with it.
Run Reports
★
Searching by day
Four day pills at the top of the Run Report screen — Mon–Thu, Friday, Saturday, Sunday. Auto-set to today. Tap any pill to search another day's services — results, segment, and times all match.
Example: On Wednesday, tap Saturday then search DG74 — you see BHI→VYS with Saturday timing, not the Monday BDT→VYS route.
★
How the app identifies your route
The second character of the train number identifies the line. Cross-network services (DY, TE, D1 etc.) show all stops origin to terminus with your guard segment highlighted. Stops outside your segment are greyed out. Tap 🔓 Unlock All if control changes your responsibilities.
★
Correcting scheduled times
Tap any dashed due time field on a stop in your segment to edit it. The delay calculation recalculates automatically. The app uses official QR Working Timetable (GTFS) data — edit if a stop shows the wrong scheduled time.
🔀
Route Change on empty services
Tap 🔀 Route Change at the bottom of the run sheet. Select the change station, enter the new train number and destination. Prior stops lock, remaining stops unlock. Up to 3 route changes per working — all included in handover.

The toolbar also has ✂️ Truncate, 📝 Manual, and ✏️ Edit buttons. Tap ⋯ More to reveal 🗺 Route Builder (build a run sheet by origin and destination — no train number needed), ⏱ Hold / Delay (add minutes to a station and every stop after it for an event hold or timetable variation — held to the terminus, shown as the new booked times with the reason pre-filled, and saveable as a named preset), 👤 Crew Handover (record where a tutor or relief guard takes the train onward — handover station, their name, service number and reason, with the option to end your report at that stop), ↻ Reload Run Sheet (refresh from timetable), and 🗑 Clear Run Sheet (remove and start over).
🚧
Altered & Special Runs
Tap 🚧 Altered on the shift card to open the Altered menu — start an ad-hoc shift, or take a saved temporary card live. Build manual run sheets from the green panel in the Run Report tab — load a saved template or add stops one by one. Each submitted run sheet is added to the Altered job card automatically.

All workings, run reports, incidents and faults save to your diary and appear in the handover when you sign off — including special runs done without Altered mode.
Special run without Altered mode? Use 📝 Build Manual Run Sheet directly from the Run Report tab — everything saves to diary and handover automatically.
Know your origin and destination but not the train number? Use 🗺 Route Builder — available from the Run Report tab idle screen or via ⋯ More on any loaded run sheet. Pick your origin and destination, optionally enter a train number and first departure time, and the app builds the full stop list from the route database automatically. Times are calculated from the database offsets if a first departure is provided.
At End of Shift
6
Sign Off — Shift Handover
Tap Sign Out → review the Shift Handover → tap Sign Off →. Your handover saves automatically.

The incoming guard sees it automatically when they log in — but only if they are picking up one of your train numbers, or you have incidents or faults to pass on. This prevents unrelated handovers appearing for guards on different services.
Shift notes reminder: If you have incidents or faults logged but no shift notes, the app will prompt you to add notes before signing off. Tap Cancel to skip and sign off without notes.
Clean shift? A green "Nothing to hand over" message appears. The incoming guard taps Accept or Skip to continue.
↻
Updating the App
The app checks for updates every 5 minutes in the background — including when pinned to your home screen. No refresh or reopen needed.

When a new version is available, a gold banner appears automatically. The ↻ Update button in the header also turns gold. Finish what you're doing, then tap ↻ Install when ready. All saved data is preserved.
Fully automatic: No supervisor notification needed. The banner appears on its own within 5 minutes of a new version being deployed.
Reporting
🔧Log a FaultReports tab
1Tap Report Fault from the home screen Quick Actions
2Select the Fault Type — RDC or ISST assigned automatically
3Select Unit Type, Unit Number, and Carriage
4Enter Train Number if known — optional
5Add description, set severity, attach photos if needed
6Tap Submit Fault Report — reference number generated automatically
After submitting: A View Submitted Faults → link appears below the submit button — tap it to go straight to your reports.
Graffiti: Cat 1–2 external fill-in · Cat 3–4 external tags · Cat 5 internal · Cat Obscene — report immediately
🚨Log an IncidentReports tab
1Tap Report Incident from Quick Actions
2Select Incident Type and fill in unit details if applicable
3Enter Train Number if known — optional
4Enter description and action taken, set priority
5Tap Submit Report
Types: Security · Medical · Safety · Infrastructure · Passenger Complaint · Other
📅Search run reports by dayRun Report tab
1Tap the Run Report tab — four day pills: Mon–Thu, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
2Today's day is pre-selected — tap any pill to switch
3Enter the train number — run sheet, segment, and times match the selected day
Example: On Wednesday tap Saturday, search DG74 — Saturday route and timing, not Monday's timetable.
🔍Look up any train's full timetableRun Report tab
1Tap the Run Report tab and select the correct day pill
2Type the train number — e.g. DP37, DG02, DP39
3The full origin-to-destination run displays with all stops and times — not just one guard's segment
4All stops are active — times are calculated from route DB sectionals anchored at the true train origin
Note: This uses job card data to determine the correct origin and destination. If a guard opens a train from their own job card button, they see their segment only. Manual search always shows the full run.

Investigating incorrect times? Use Dev Mode → Workings tab to dump all job card entries for that train, then check Search Log to see exactly what data was used. If timetable search is correct but job card is wrong, the issue is in the job card working string for that specific card.
✏️Edit a scheduled due timeRun Report tab
1Load a run sheet — due times appear as dashed fields on stops in your segment
2Tap the due time field to edit — enter the correct 4-digit time
3The +/− delay column recalculates automatically
When to use: If the scheduled time is wrong for that service or day — correct before entering departure time.
✏️Edit station list & save to Job CardRun Report tab
1Load a run sheet for your train number
2Tap ✏️ Edit in the run sheet footer — the Edit Run Sheet panel opens
3To remove a stop — tap the ✕ next to the station name
4To add a stop — select a station from the dropdown at the bottom and tap ＋ Add
5To reorder stops — use the ▲ ▼ arrows next to each station
6To edit a due time — tap the Due field next to the station and enter a 4-digit time
7Tap 💾 Save Changes to Job Card — the updated station list saves permanently and survives sign-out
When to use: Trackwork causing a different route (e.g. Bindha → Bald Hills), empty workings with wrong stops, or any service where the loaded station list doesn't match the actual run.
⚠️ Recorded action. Every edit requires a reason and your acknowledgement. Your name, service number, job card, original station list, changes made, and reason are permanently logged and visible to supervisors. Only edit when operationally necessary.
✎Edit header details & the 📋 working noteRun Report tab
1Load the run sheet, then tap the small ✎ Edit button next to the train number at the top
2Pick a reason (recorded action), then the Edit Header Details panel opens
3Change the Train Number, Route Description, Your Segment, Driver or Unit Numbers
4Use the Working Note field to set the 📋 line under the train number — e.g. EXP MJE to MNY, REL or FINAL DEST
5Tap 💾 Save Header Changes — the note and all header details save permanently and survive sign-out
This is how you add an express pattern to a job card: type the express notation into Working Note (e.g. EXP MJE to MNY) and save. Clear the field and save to remove the note.
⚠️ Recorded action. Header edits are logged with your name and service number and are visible to supervisors. Only edit when operationally necessary.
Use the Preset Pattern picker when the timetable loads the wrong route for a train — for example, a cross-network service showing incorrect stops.
1Search for the train number on the correct day
2Tap ⋯ More at the bottom of the run sheet toolbar
3Tap 📋 Load Preset Pattern
4Enter the first departure time as 4 digits (e.g. 1118 for 11:18)
5Select the correct route from the list and tap ✅ Load This Pattern
6All stops and times load automatically. Use ✏️ Edit to fine-tune individual times if needed
Times are approximate for cross-network services, based on standard running times. Always verify against your working timetable. The pattern saves automatically for that train and day so it loads correctly next time.
⚠️ Recorded action. Every preset load is permanently logged with your name, service number, job card, train number, route applied, and timestamp. This is visible to supervisors. Only use when operationally necessary and the timetable data is incorrect.
⏱️Submit a Late Running ReportRun Report tab
1Tap the Run Report tab
2Enter the train number — or it auto-loads your current working
3Full stop list loads — your segment highlighted, other stops greyed out
4Enter actual departure time at each stop in your segment
5Select a delay reason for each late stop — if the reason hasn't changed, tap ↑ same to copy the previous stop's reason automatically
6Tap Submit Late Running Report
Tip: Enter driver name and unit numbers in the header. Record times as you go — you can submit mid-trip.
🔀Log a Route ChangeRun Report tab
1Tap 🔀 Route Change at the bottom of the run sheet
2Select the station where the change occurred
3Enter the new train number given by control
4Select the new destination — tap "Other station…" if not listed
5Tap Confirm Change — prior stops lock, remaining stops unlock
6Record departure times and submit as normal
Up to 3 route changes per working — all included in the handover.
🚫Record a Closed or Skipped StationRun Report tab
1Load the run sheet — locate the station that is closed or being skipped
2Tap the reason dropdown for that stop and select 🚫 Station closed or ⏭ Station skipped from the top of the list
3The departure time field disables automatically — no time entry needed
4The stop dot turns amber to flag it visually on the run sheet
5Submit as normal — the stop appears in the report with amber styling and no delay penalty
Station skipped is used when control instructs you to bypass stops to recover late running. Station closed is used for scheduled closures (e.g. refurbishment). Both are recorded in the run report for the supervisor.
Altered & Special Workings
✂️Truncate a Route (Early Termination)
1Load the run sheet for your train number
2Tap ✂️ Truncate in the run sheet footer
3Select the last stop in service
4Tap ✂️ Truncate Route — all stops beyond are removed
5Record departure times and submit as normal
⏱Hold / Timetable Variation
1Load the run sheet, then tap ⋯ More in the footer → ⏱ Hold / Delay
2Pick the station the hold starts from and enter the minutes to add (e.g. +2)
3Tap Apply Hold — the time is added to that stop and every stop after it, held to the terminus
4Held stops show the new booked times (not as lateness), with the reason set to Timetable variation automatically
5Record actual departure times against the held times and submit as normal
Save a preset: name the hold (e.g. "Suncorp NRL +2 from RST") and tap Save to reuse it on any matching run sheet from the same sheet. A full reload from the timetable clears an applied hold, so re-apply the preset if needed. Presets are stored on this device.
👤Crew Handover (tutor / relief guard)
1On the run sheet tap ⋯ More in the footer → 👤 Crew Handover
2Pick the handover station, enter the relief / tutor guard's name, service number and a short reason / note (e.g. "Tutor taking train to airport")
3Choose End my run report at this stop — Yes greys out the stops after the handover (they become the relief guard's), No keeps all stops on your report
4Tap Confirm Handover — a purple note appears on the run sheet header and on the submitted run report
5The handover is saved on the run report, in your diary, in the sign-off handover and in the diary export
Tutor working the whole run? If you enter no departure times of your own, the submit bar shows 📤 Submit Handover Record so you can still log the run with the handover note — you no longer need a time to submit.
Made a mistake? Reopen ⋯ More → 👤 Crew Handover and tap Remove to clear the note — any stops that were greyed out come back. The note survives a run sheet reload.
📝Manual Run Sheet & Templates
1Run Report tab → tap 📝 Build Manual Run Sheet in the green Altered panel
2Check Saved Templates — tap to load all stops instantly if your service matches
3If no template, add stops one by one in order
4Enter the train number and first stop departure time
5Tap Build Run Sheet — times calculated for every stop
6Record actual departure times and submit — report saves to diary and handover automatically
Templates: VYS→Banoon · Banoon→VYS · VYS→Beenleigh · BNH→VYS · HLN→VYS · VYS→HLN
🚧Altered Job Card — Auto Build
1Tap 🚧 Altered → Start Ad-hoc Altered Shift — shift card turns amber
2Build run sheets and record times as normal for each working
3Each submitted run sheet is added automatically to the Altered job card
4Tap View Job Card → to see it building in real time
5Tap ✏️ Edit to add meal breaks and sign off time
6At sign-off, the 🚧 Altered Job Card appears at the top of the handover
Activated by mistake? Tap 🚧 Altered ON again — if no workings have been logged yet, a Cancel Altered option appears. This restores the shift display to normal. Once workings are logged the cancel option is not available — tap to end the shift normally instead.
🚧Temporary (Altered) Cards — Plan Ahead
1Tap 🚧 Altered → ＋ New Temporary Card
2Enter the base job card — auto-named with a T suffix (EB323 → EB323T). Set the altered sign on/off and any meal breaks → 💾 Save Temporary Card
3Build run reports in advance — tap ＋ Run report on the card to open the Route Builder; pick origin → destination, set the times, then tap 💾 Save run sheet in the amber bar. Add as many as you need, then Done
4On the day, tap 🚧 Altered → ▶ Go Live on the card — your shift hours switch to the card's, and its run reports appear as amber pills on the Run Report screen
5Tap a pill to open that run report and record the actual times, then submit as normal
6Tap 🗑 on the card to delete it (double confirmation) — any time up to the shift end if you make a mistake, or once the block is over
Up to 3 cards. Saved cards survive sign-out and carry across a multi-day block. Deleting a card never affects your diary.
Boarding, Roster & Contacts
🌦️Check BOM weatherHome screen
1On the home screen, tap the 🌦️ BOM Weather button in Reference & Tools
2The Bureau of Meteorology forecast page opens in Safari
♿Acknowledge a Boarding Assist AlertBoarding tab
1An alert sounds and a red banner appears at the top of the screen automatically
2Tap ✓ ACK on the banner, or tap the Boarding tab → ♿ Receive Alerts to see full details — unit, carriage, door position, passenger type
3Tap ✓ Acknowledge on the alert card to confirm you have received and actioned the request
Tip: The alarm banner stays at the top and repeats every 20 seconds until acknowledged — you won't miss it.
📤Send a Boarding Assist RequestBoarding tab
1Tap the Boarding tab → tap 📤 Send Request
2Select the Assist Type — Wheelchair, Vision, Pram, Mobility Aid, Hearing, or Other
3Enter the Train Number and Unit No. — auto-fill from your current run sheet if one is loaded
4Select Carriage and Door Position — tap 1st Door or 2nd Door (visible from the platform)
5Select the station under Assisted Boarding At and enter the Passenger Destination
6If the passenger needs assistance alighting, tick ✓ Assistance also required at destination
7Add any Additional Notes if needed, then tap 📤 Send Boarding Request
8The request appears in Sent This Session — tap ✅ Station Attended or ❌ Unattended when the ABO responds
Tip: Train number, unit, and door position are remembered between sends on the same service.
🔄Swap a ShiftMy Roster
1Tap My Roster from Quick Actions
2Tap the day you are swapping
3Use the Depot buttons (All · Robina · Beenleigh) to filter the job card list — your depot is pre-selected
4Select the new job card from the list
5Tap Regular for a normal swap or Overtime for an OT shift
6Tap Save — home screen shows original with strikethrough, new card with SWAP or OT badge
To undo: Tap the day again → tap Clear.
✏️One-off Card & TimesMy Roster
1Use this when you work a one-off card or amended times that are not in the job-card list — without changing your rostered card
2Tap My Roster → tap the day → tap ✏️ 1-Off in the Type row
3Type the job card number and the amended Sign On and Sign Off times
4Choose Regular or 🟢 Overtime, then tap Save
5The day shows a 1-OFF badge (green 1-OFF OT for overtime), the crossed-out rostered card and your amended times, on the roster and the Today card
Display only: It does not record annual or sick leave and does not change train search, routes or run sheets. Tap the day → Clear to revert to rostered.
↻Refresh the shift display
1If the shift banner shows the wrong job card, tap the small ↻ button next to ⇄ Swap on the shift banner
2The app re-reads your roster, saved swaps, and OT — and recalculates the correct card for today
3A green ✓ Shift display refreshed toast confirms it ran
Safe any time: Only updates the shift banner — all reports and run sheets are unaffected.
🆕Install an app update
1The app checks for updates every 5 minutes in the background — works on home screen PWA, no refresh needed
2When a new version is available, a gold banner appears automatically at the top of the screen
3The ↻ Update button in the header also turns gold — visible at a glance
4Finish what you are doing — the banner does not force an update mid-shift
5Tap ↻ Install on the banner when ready — app reloads and logs back in, all data preserved
Fully automatic: No supervisor notification needed. Banner appears on its own within 5 minutes of a new version being deployed.
Diary & Export
✏️Edit an Incident or Fault Report
1Tap 📓 Diary → find the shift → tap the incident or fault entry to expand it
2Tap ✏️ Edit — the edit form opens with the existing details pre-filled
3Update the description, type, notes, or severity as needed
4For incidents — add or update Action Taken to record what you did
5Add a Follow-up Note if there's additional information after the fact — visible to your supervisor
6Tap ✓ Save Changes — the diary updates immediately
Also editable from Reports: Tap ✏️ Edit on any report in your current session, before signing off.
✏️Edit or Delete a Run Report
1Tap 📓 Diary → find the shift → tap the entry to expand it, then tap ✏️ Edit under the run report (or tap ✏️ Edit on a report in your current session before signing off)
2Adjust the actual departure time and reason for any stop — delays recalculate automatically
3Tap ✓ Save Changes — the diary updates immediately
4To remove the report instead, tap 🗑 Delete Run Report at the bottom of the edit form
5Confirm twice — the second prompt warns it cannot be undone — and the run report is removed
Tip: Delete is for fixing a mistake (e.g. a report logged on the wrong train). The two-step confirmation protects against an accidental tap.
⚠Duplicate Report Warning
1If you submit an incident or fault with the same type and train number as one logged in the last 30 minutes, a warning appears
2If you submit a late running report for a train you've already reported this session, a warning also appears
3Tap Cancel to go back and check — or OK to submit anyway if it's genuinely a separate event
Only triggers when a train number is entered — incidents without a train number won't show this warning.
📓Export a shift diary entry
1Tap 📓 Diary on the home screen
2Find the shift → tap 📤 Export at the bottom of the entry
3The export panel opens with a full text summary — job card, incidents, faults, late running, boarding assists, shift notes
4Tap 📋 Copy to copy to clipboard — paste into an email or message
5Or tap ↗ Share to open the iOS share sheet and send via Mail, Messages, or any other app
Tip: Use this to support formal reports or keep a personal record of your shift.
Contact Numbers
🚒 Triple Zero000
🚨 RMC Emergency1800 079 303
📡 TMO3072 8830
🔒 SMO3072 5550
🎛️ Asst Controller3072 8848
👮 Police Link131 444
📅 Shift Roster3072 8379
📋 Guards Roster3072 9251
🔄 Driver & Guard3072 9150
📞 TSD Ops 24/73072 9211
🔧 ISST (NGR faults)3072 8852
🔧 RDC (SMU / IMU)3072 8851
⚡ ECO (Electrical Control Officer)3072 1212
🚨 ECO Emergency (25kV overhead)3072 5151
Quick Reference
Graffiti: Cat 1–2 external fill-in · Cat 3–4 external tags · Cat 5 internal · Cat Obscene — report immediately
Fault reporting: NGR → ISST 3072 8852 · All others → RDC 3072 8851
Toilet: IMU (all) ✓ · NGR ✓×2 · SMU ✗
Walk times: Min 5 min all stations · Roma St 10 min · Ipswich/Yard 5–8 min · Kippa-Ring/Yard 5–20 min · Wulkuraka 5–25 min · Mayne/YNY 10–20 min
UTC zones: UTC1 Indooroopilly/Corinda · UTC2 Springfield/Salisbury · UTC3 Caboolture/Kippa-Ring · UTC6 Beenleigh/Gold Coast · UTC7 Nambour/Gympie · UTC9 Ipswich/Rosewood · UTC10 Cleveland/Wynnum
Scheduled times: Uses official QR timetable (GTFS) — tap dashed due time field to correct if wrong
Offline: App works offline — reports save and sync when back online. Red banner = offline.
Altered & Special Runs
Start: Tap 🚧 Altered → Start Ad-hoc Altered Shift → enter job card, sign on/off, meal breaks → Start Altered Shift
Activated by mistake? Tap 🚧 Altered ON again (before any workings logged) → Cancel Altered
Each working: Build Manual Run Sheet → record times → submit — added to the Altered job card automatically
End shift: Tap 🚧 Altered ON → End Altered Shift → enter actual sign-off time → Close Shift & Save to Diary
Special run (no Altered mode): Use 📝 Build Manual Run Sheet directly — saves to diary and handover automatically

Temporary cards (e.g. EB323T): For a multi-day trackwork block where your rostered card has altered hours, tap 🚧 Altered → ＋ New Temporary Card and save the adjusted sign on/off and meals. The card is auto-named with a T suffix (EB323 → EB323T). Build run reports in advance by tapping ＋ Run report on the card — this opens the Route Builder with an amber bar at the bottom; pick origin and destination, set the times, then tap 💾 Save run sheet to attach it (add as many as you need, then tap Done). On the day, tap 🚧 Altered → ▶ Go Live to load it — it overrides your shift hours while Altered is on. You can delete a card at any time up to its shift end time (with a double confirmation) if you make a mistake. Tap 🗑 to delete it when the block is over; your diary is never affected.
Hold / timetable variation: On the run sheet tap ⋯ More → ⏱ Hold / Delay, pick the start station and minutes (e.g. +2 from Roma St for a Suncorp event) → Apply Hold. The time is added to that stop and every stop after it to the terminus, shown as the new booked times (not as lateness) with the reason set to Timetable variation. Save it as a named preset to reuse on any matching run sheet; a full timetable reload clears an applied hold. Presets are stored on this device.
Crew handover: On the run sheet tap ⋯ More → 👤 Crew Handover, pick the handover station, enter the relief / tutor guard name, service number and reason, and choose whether your report ends at that stop (rest greys out as theirs) or keeps all stops → Confirm Handover. Shows on the run report, in your diary and in the handover; reopen and tap Remove to clear it.
Altered Run Report (report tagging)
What it does: A standalone toggle on the Run Report screen, separate from Altered shifts and temporary cards. Its only job is to change the job-card number stamped on the reports you submit.
Use it for: a one-off working booked on a different card number, when you don't need to start a full Altered shift or set up a temporary card.
How: tap ⚡ Altered Report, type the card number, then search and report trains as normal. Every incident, fault, late-running and boarding report is then filed under that number instead of your rostered card. Tap Off to revert.
Tagging only: trains, routes, times and the run sheet are unchanged. It does not start a shift, load workings or save a shift to your diary.
Signing Off
Tap Sign Out → review Shift Handover → tap Sign Off →
Handover saves automatically — incoming guard sees it on their next login
Incoming guard: handover sheet slides up after login → tap ✓ Accept Handover
Swap a shift: My Roster → tap day → select card → Regular or Overtime → Save
One-off card/times: tap day → ✏️ 1-Off → enter card number, sign on/off → Regular or Overtime → Save
Undo swap: tap the day again → Clear
App Updates
App checks for updates every 5 minutes in background — works on home screen PWA, no refresh needed
Gold banner appears automatically — ↻ Update button in header also turns gold
Finish your current task — banner won't interrupt mid-shift
Tap ↻ Install when ready — reloads and logs back in, all data preserved
Diary Export
Tap 📓 Diary → find shift → tap 📤 Export
📋 Copy — copies full summary to clipboard → paste into email or message
↗ Share — opens iOS share sheet (Mail, Messages etc.)
Includes: job card · incidents · faults · late running · boarding assists · shift notes
Diary Backups (Restore)
Automatic: before any save that would lose diary content, the previous state is backed up (last 10 kept)
🛟 Restore — tap in the My Diary header → choose a backup by time → Restore
Restoring backs up your current diary first, so it is reversible

One-off amended card & times for this day only. Leave the card list above unused.

Full Report
🗂️ Select Your Job Card
🔍
New Incident Report
🚑 Medical Emergency — Quick Reference
1. Stop the train if safe to do so at next station
2. Call RMC — 1800 079 303 — give train no., location, nature of incident
3. PA announcement: "Ladies and gentlemen, we are currently attending to a passenger in need of assistance. We apologise for any delay."
4. Request ambulance via RMC — give: location, patient age/gender, symptoms, consciousness level
5. Keep passengers clear — ask if anyone has first aid training
6. Do not move patient unless in immediate danger
7. Meet ambulance at platform — advise exact carriage
📞 Call RMC Emergency — 1800 079 303
📷
Tap to add photos
Up to 6 images

👥 Manage Users
You'll be asked to select the TRAINS index.html.
Select the latest file Claude gave you, then save the result to GitHub.
Guards access the shift console. Supervisors access the dashboard only.
Bulk Import from CSV
CSV format: name,service,pin,depot,role
e.g. Alex Johnson,123456,1234,ROB,guard
Select the guard and set a new temporary 4-digit PIN. They will be prompted to change it on next login.
Active notices on this device. Guards who sign in here will see them at login. Tap Delete to remove a notice — it clears immediately for any guard currently signed in.
Bulk PIN Reset
Reset all guards in a depot to the same temporary PIN. They will each be prompted to set their own PIN on next login.
Bulk Notice
Send a notice to all guards in a specific depot.

🔀 Route Change

Log a new train number and destination. Stops from the change station will unlock for time entry.

👤 Crew Handover

Record where another guard (e.g. a tutor or relief) takes the train onward. Logged on the run report and in your diary/handover.

Set this when you take the train back later — the from→to stops are greyed as theirs and the rest stays open.

✏️ Edit Late Running Report

✂️ Trackwork — Truncate Route

Select the last stop in service. All stops after this point will be removed from the run sheet.

⏱ Hold / Timetable Variation

Adds the held minutes to the booked time of the chosen station and every stop after it, holding the variation to the terminus. Shown as the new booked times (not as late), with the reason set to Timetable variation.
Saved Presets
Saves the station and minutes selected above as a reusable preset.

✎ Edit Header Details

⚠️ Recorded action. Your name, service number, and all changes made here are permanently logged and visible to supervisors. Only edit when operationally necessary.
Train Number
Route Description
Working Note — the 📋 line under the train number (e.g. EXP MJE-MNY, REL, FINAL DEST)
Your Segment — shown in the gold banner
Driver Name
Unit Numbers
✏️
Reason for Editing
Required before changes can be made
⚠️ This action is recorded. Your name, service number, job card, your changes, and this reason will be logged and visible to supervisors.

✏️ Edit Run Sheet Stations

Edit station list and due times. Changes save permanently to your job card.

Build a run sheet from any origin to any destination using the route database — no train number required.

Outward Contact Process
Unattended Station — Customer Assist
♿
Customer
Loading…
📞
Step 1 — Outward Contact Call
Active
👤
Customer
—
Not called
Guard says:
"Hello, is that the customer? This is [your name], the Guard on train —. I'm calling to let you know I'm approaching — and I'm aware you require assistance to board. I'll be deploying the ramp — are you on the platform now?"
📋 Identify yourself and your train number. Confirm the station. Check the customer is ready and on the platform before arrival.
Call outcome:
✅
Answered — Ready
⏳
Answered — Not Ready
📵
No Answer
💬
Left Voicemail
🚉
Step 2 — Advise Boarding Position
Pending
Guard says:
"When I arrive at —, please make your way to Door 1, Car 1 at the front of the train. I'll deploy the ramp — please don't attempt to board without me. I'll be there in approximately a few minutes."
📋 Confirm the exact door and car. Remind the customer not to board unassisted. Give an ETA so they know how long to wait.
⏱ ETA to station
—
Skip
🔁
Step 3 — No Answer / Escalation
Pending
⚠️ If no answer: Attempt a second call 2 minutes before arrival. If still no answer, proceed to the platform and make a PA announcement to alert the customer.
PA Announcement (if no contact):
"Attention passengers at — station — this is an announcement for the passenger requiring mobility assistance. Your ramp is now deployed at Door 1, Car 1. Please make your way to the front of the platform. This train will be held briefly."
Select escalation action:
🔄
Retry Call
📢
PA Announcement
📻
Advise Control
🚆
Proceed — Not Found
N/A — Skip
♿
Step 4 — Ramp Deployment & Boarding
Pending
🛠 Proceed to the agreed door. Greet the customer. Deploy ramp. Assist boarding. Stow ramp. Confirm destination.
Guard says:
"Welcome aboard — I'm [your name], we spoke on the phone. Let me get the ramp set up. Once on board the designated wheelchair space is just inside the door. We'll be departing shortly."
Confirm all completed:
Skip
🏁
Step 5 — Alighting & Close
Pending
📍 Advise the customer 1–2 stops before alighting. Deploy ramp on arrival. Confirm customer is safely on the platform before departing.
Guard says (approaching destination):
"We're approaching your destination, which is the next stop. I'll get the ramp ready for you now — please make your way to Door 1 when ready."
Notes / observations:
✅ Outward Contact Record Saved

Notes
Supervisor Help
Step-by-step guide
Signing In & Out
🔑Log in as Supervisor
1Open the app — tap the 🔒 Supervisor Login button below the login card
2Your service number and PIN fill automatically — tap Sign In →
3The Supervisor Dashboard opens directly
Own credentials? Enter your personal service number and PIN manually instead.
🚪Sign Out
1Tap Sign Out in the top-right corner — returns to login immediately
Shared device? Always sign out before handing to a guard.
Dashboard Controls
↻Header Buttons
1VIEW ⊟ — toggle compact / full guard status cards
2NOTICE 📢 — post a message to guards on this device
3REFRESH ↻ — manually refresh all dashboard data immediately. Dashboard auto-refreshes every 60 seconds
4REPORTS ↗ — open the combined incident/fault/late running export panel
5GUARDS 👥 — manage guard accounts, reset PINs, add/remove guards
6HELP ? — this help panel
7Sign Out — return to the login screen immediately
📅Filter by Date
1Tap Today, Yesterday, This Week, All, or Custom from the date pills
2Yesterday is useful for reviewing the previous shift handover without needing Custom
3For custom — set the From and To dates — dashboard updates automatically
🏭Filter by Depot
1Tap All Depots, Robina, Beenleigh, Ipswich, Petrie, or Ferny Grove from the depot pills
2All stats and records update to show only that depot
3Your depot filter choice is saved automatically — it restores when you log back in
⚠View Unresolved Items
1Each incident and fault row now shows a coloured status pill — amber = Logged/Open · red = Escalated · green = Closed
2Unresolved records have an amber left border so they stand out at a glance
3Tap ⚠ Unresolved in the Incidents or Faults filter bar to show only items still needing action
🔥Incident Hotspot Analysis
1Scroll to the 🔥 Incident Hotspots section — shows bar charts of the most-reported train numbers, incident/fault types, and guards for the current filter period
2Use with the date filter to spot patterns across a week or month — useful for identifying recurring trains, routes, or shift patterns that generate more events
Near-Miss incidents also appear in hotspot data — guards can now log near-miss events as a separate incident type to help identify safety patterns before they escalate.
✓Update Incident or Fault Status
1Tap any incident or fault row to expand it
2Type a supervisor note in the text field (optional) — saved to the guard's diary alongside the status change
3Tap Escalate, Close, or Reopen — the status updates immediately and the dashboard refreshes
High severity incidents pulse with a red glow border so they stand out even in a long list.
Follow-up notes added by the guard after editing appear highlighted in the detail panel.
⏱View Late Running Detail
1Tap any late running row to expand it
2The full stop-by-stop table appears — Station, Due, Dep, +/−, and Reason for every stop
3Route, driver name, and unit numbers shown above the table where available
Total delay minutes shown in amber below the Late Run count on the stat card — e.g. ⏱ 23 min total.
Guard name pills in each section show a count badge — tap a guard's pill to filter that section to their reports only.
▼Collapse & Expand Sections
1Tap the header of any section — Incidents, Fault Reports, Late Running, or Assisted Boarding — to collapse it
2The arrow changes from ▼ to ▶ and the content hides — tap again to expand
Boarding: A red X pending badge appears on the Assisted Boarding header when there are unacknowledged assists.
📈7-Day Trend Sparklines
1A small trend line appears under each stat card — Incidents, Faults, Late Running, and Boarding
2Line colour shows the trend — red = increasing · green = decreasing · neutral = flat
3The filled dot marks today's value — hover or tap the stat card to read the number
⏱Set Session Auto-Lock
1The Auto-lock after slider sits at the top of the dashboard
2Drag to set between 2 – 30 minutes — you'll be signed out automatically after this period of inactivity
3A 60-second warning appears before logout — tap I'm still here to stay signed in
Your setting saves automatically and persists between sessions.
🚨High Severity Incident Alerts
1When a guard submits a High severity incident, a red 🚨 alert banner appears at the top of your dashboard automatically
2The banner shows the incident type, train number, guard name, time, and description
3Tap ✕ on an alert to dismiss it once actioned — dismissed alerts don't return
Persists across refreshes until you dismiss it — you won't miss a high severity event even if you step away.
↗Reports
1Apply your date and depot filters, then tap ↗ in the header — a reports menu appears
2↗ Full Incident & Fault Report — complete details for all incidents, faults, late running (with stop-by-stop tables), and boarding — shared as plain text email
3📋 Shift Summary by Guard — one row per guard with counts, total delay, and High severity flags
4⏱ On-Time Performance — delay stats, reason breakdown with bar chart, and worst services table
5Tap ↗ Share in the report overlay to send via email, Messages, or save as PDF
All three reports respect the current date and depot filter selections.
Reading the Board
👁Check Guard Activity
1The Guard Status Board sits at the top of the dashboard, above the stats
2Each card shows the guard's name, job card, current train, shift times, today's report badges, and how long since they last used the app
3Colour coding — green = active · amber = idle · grey = offline
4Tap any card to expand full details — then use the three quick-action buttons: 📢 Notice to send a message, 🔑 Reset PIN to reset their PIN, 📓 Diary to view today's reports without leaving the dashboard
5Tap ⊟ in the header to switch to compact mode — cards collapse to a single line showing name, status dot, job card, and current train
Live ticker: A rotating bar below the guard board shows the most recent report across all guards — guard name · event · time.
Auto-refreshes every 60 seconds alongside the rest of the dashboard.
⏱Set the Idle Threshold
1At the top of the Guard Status Board, drag the Mark idle after slider
2Range is 5 – 60 minutes in 5-minute steps — guards inactive longer than this show as amber
3Your chosen threshold saves automatically and persists between sessions
Offline vs Idle: Guards inactive more than 90 minutes show as grey regardless of the threshold setting.
Managing Guards
👥Add a New Guard or Supervisor
1Tap 👥 in the header → tap the + Add tab
2Enter full name, 6-digit service number, and 4-digit PIN
3Select the role — tap 🛡 Guard or ⭐ Supervisor — the depot field hides automatically for supervisors
4For guards, select the depot — Robina or Beenleigh
5Tap Add User — appears in the list immediately on this device
6Tap ↗ Export Updated App to make permanent across all devices
Important: Export and push to GitHub — all devices pick up the change within 5 minutes.
🔑Reset a Guard's PIN
1Tap 👥 → find the guard in the Users list → tap their row to expand
2Tap Reset PIN → enter a new temporary 4-digit PIN → tap Save
3Guard is prompted to set their own PIN on next login
Different device? Export and redeploy first so the reset takes effect everywhere.
🗑Remove a Guard
1Tap 👥 → find the guard → tap their row to expand
2Tap Delete and confirm
3Tap ↗ Export Updated App to make permanent across all devices
⚡Bulk PIN Reset
1Tap 👥 → tap the Bulk tab
2Select All Depots, Robina, or Beenleigh
3Enter a temporary 4-digit PIN → tap Reset PINs for Selected Depot
4All guards in that depot are reset — each is prompted to set their own PIN on next login
Use after a security incident to force all guards to choose new PINs immediately.
📢Bulk Notice by Depot
1Tap 👥 → tap the Bulk tab → scroll down to Bulk Notice
2Select the depot and type your message → tap 📢 Send to Depot
3Notice is delivered to all guards in that depot — read receipts appear in the 📢 notice modal
🔑Change Your Own PIN
1Tap ? in the header → tap 🔑 Change my PIN
2Enter your current PIN, then your new PIN twice to confirm
3Tap Save New PIN — takes effect immediately
Notice Board
📢Send a Notice to Guards
1Tap the 📢 button in the header
2Select All Guards or a specific guard from the dropdown
3Type your message → tap 📢 Post Notice
4The notice appears as a pinned banner on the guard's home screen at next login or within 60 seconds
Banner stays pinned until the guard taps Acknowledge — they cannot dismiss it without confirming.
✓Check Who Has Read a Notice
1Tap 📢 — your sent notices are listed below the compose area
2Each notice shows a X/Y seen pill — grey = none read · amber = some read · green = all read
3Tap the notice to see a per-guard breakdown — each guard's name and the exact time they acknowledged
✕Recall a Notice
1Tap 📢 — find the notice in the sent list
2Tap Recall — the notice is removed from all guard screens on their next refresh
🔑 Change My PIN
Supervisor Report
Reports
⏱️ Session expires in ~5 minutes — tap anywhere to stay signed in
⏱
Session timing out
You'll be signed out in
60
seconds
Developer Access
Enter PIN to continue
🛠 DEV MODE ACTIVE — tap to open panel
🛠 Developer Panel
Loading…

📌 Load Preset Pattern

Select the correct route pattern, enter the first departure time, and the full stopping pattern will load automatically.
First Departure Time
Route Pattern
